mappings.rationale: Directly covers British history since 1920 including the interwar depression, WWII under Churchill, the welfare state, and Thatcher's government discussed in subsection A.1.a.
Relevant to the political developments in Britain including Labour and Conservative governments, devolution, and the Common Market discussed in subsection A.1.a.
Relevant to British constitutional developments including devolved assemblies for Scotland and Wales discussed in subsection A.1.a.iv.
Directly covers Northern Ireland since 1922, the Catholic-Protestant antagonism, violence, and the Belfast Agreement discussed in subsection A.1.b.ii.
Directly relevant to the establishment of the Irish Free State, the division of Ireland, and political developments in Ireland since 1920 discussed in subsection A.1.b.
Directly covers French history including the Third Republic, Vichy government, Fourth and Fifth Republics, and de Gaulle discussed in subsection A.2.
Provides the historical foundation for understanding French political culture and the left-right conflicts that continued into the 20th century in subsection A.2.
Directly covers the rise of National Socialism, the Third Reich, Hitler's totalitarian state, persecution of Jews, and WWII discussed in subsection A.3.a.ii.
Directly relevant to Germany's reaction to the Treaty of Versailles, reparations, and the political instability that contributed to the rise of the Nazis discussed in subsection A.3.a.i.
Directly covers Italian history since 1920 including the Fascist era, Mussolini, postwar recovery, and political developments discussed in subsection A.4.
Directly relevant to the rise of fascism in Italy under Mussolini, the Fascist Party's rise to power, and the fascist dictatorships in Spain and Portugal discussed in subsections A.4 and A.5.
Directly covers the Spanish Civil War, German and Italian intervention, and Franco's victory discussed in subsection A.5.a.i.
Directly covers the Soviet Union from the communist state through Gorbachev's reforms and the dissolution of the USSR discussed in subsection B.1.
Provides broader Russian historical context for understanding Soviet and post-Soviet developments discussed in subsection B.1.
Provides background for the establishment of the communist state that is the starting point for the Soviet Union's history discussed in subsection B.1.a.
Relevant to Russian political developments after the collapse of the Soviet Union including the Commonwealth of Independent States discussed in subsection B.1.c.
Relevant to Soviet economic issues including War Communism, the New Economic Policy, agricultural problems, and the economic crisis under Gorbachev discussed in subsection B.1.
Directly relevant to the purges and political repression under Stalin discussed in subsection B.1.a.ii.
Relevant to the communist governments established throughout eastern Europe and their eventual collapse discussed in subsections B.1 and B.2-3.
Provides essential background for understanding the successor states of the Habsburg Empire including Austria, Hungary, Czechoslovakia, and the Balkans discussed in subsections B.2 and B.3.
Directly relevant to the European Economic Community, Common Market, and EU expansion including membership of eastern European states discussed throughout the section.
Relevant to the legal framework of European integration and the European Economic Community discussed throughout the section.
Relevant to the democratic transitions in eastern Europe, the fall of communist regimes, and the establishment of parliamentary governments discussed throughout the section.
Relevant to the Labour governments and welfare state in Britain, Social Democratic parties in Scandinavia, and socialist movements across Europe discussed throughout.
Directly relevant to the establishment of the welfare state in Britain (subsection A.1.a.iv), Scandinavian welfare states (A.6.b.iii), and social welfare programs across western Europe.

mappings.vsiTitle: The Great Depression and The New Deal
mappings.rationale: Relevant to the Great Depression's impact on European economies discussed across multiple national subsections including Germany, France, Scandinavia, and the Balkans.
Relevant to the wartime experiences of all European nations discussed throughout the section, including occupation, resistance, and liberation.
Relevant to the persecution of Jews and the concentration camp system under Nazi Germany discussed in subsection A.3.a.ii.
Relevant to the nationalist movements including Basque separatism, Scottish and Welsh devolution, Walloon-Fleming division, and ethnic conflicts in the Balkans discussed throughout.
Relevant to the rise of populist and extremist movements in the interwar period and their role in destabilizing democratic governments across Europe.
Relevant to the authoritarian regimes discussed including Franco's Spain, Salazar's Portugal, military dictatorships in Greece, and communist dictatorships in eastern Europe.
Relevant to the numerous revolutions and political upheavals including the Hungarian Revolution (1956), the Prague Spring (1968), and the 1989 revolutions across eastern Europe.
Relevant to the civil wars discussed including the Spanish Civil War, Greek civil war (1946-49), and the Yugoslav wars of the 1990s.
Relevant to the federal structures discussed including the Belgian federalization along communal lines and the federal arrangements in Germany and Switzerland.
Relevant to the devolved assembly for Scotland discussed in subsection A.1.a.iv.
Directly relevant to the persecution of Jews under Nazi Germany and the broader European context of antisemitism discussed in subsection A.3.a.ii.
Relevant to the context of Jewish persecution in Europe and the resulting emigration and Zionist movement that informed postwar European politics.
Provides the intellectual foundation for understanding the communist and socialist movements that shaped eastern European politics discussed in subsections B.1-3.
Relevant to the liberal democratic political tradition that shaped western European postwar development and the contrast with communist eastern Europe.
Directly relevant to the division of Europe, NATO, the Berlin Wall, and the eventual end of the Cold War discussed throughout subsections B.1-3.
Relevant to the displacement of peoples during WWII, the flight of East Germans to the West, and the Balkan refugee crises discussed throughout the section.
Relevant to France as a nuclear power and the nuclear dimension of Cold War European security discussed in the broader context of European defense.
Relevant to the ideological conflicts between fascism, communism, and liberal democracy that defined 20th-century European politics.
Provides historical background for understanding the political fragmentation and reunification of Germany discussed in subsection A.3.
Directly covers Europe since 1920 including Cold War, recovery, end of European empires, and postwar social transformations — the core subject of this section.
